title: Command Adapter Generation Contract
goal: Implement #358 by defining a minimal implementation-independent command adapter generation contract that reuses existing CLI command, operation, primitive, schema, and conformance contracts.
non_goals: Generate CLI adapter code in this slice.
Move runtime primitive implementation into contracts.

intent.outcome: A schema-validated contract shape exists that a later generator can consume for one read-only command adapter.
intent.constraints: Stay on master; keep shipped package repo-/agent-/tool-/language-agnostic; reuse existing contract surfaces instead of duplicating them.
intent.latitude: Choose whether the generation contract is a new small manifest plus schema or an extension of existing contract files.
intent.escalation: Stop before generating adapter code or hard-coding Python-only assumptions.

execution.next_step: Inspect contract loading, schemas, and tests, then add the minimal generation contract and validation coverage.
execution.proof: Use proof --changed, contract tooling checks, focused tests, planning checks, reconcile, summary.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Generated shipped CLI adapters from authoritative package definitions.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #358 closes with a generation contract sufficient for root `defaults`.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: A generator can consume a single command-adapter contract instead of rediscovering command, option, operation, and primitive fields.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Generated adapter implementation, drift guardrails, package CLI extension, and multi-adapter projection requirements.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Validated contract-consumption parity still requires a separate hard-coded expected-consumer update; #364 records that dogfooding friction.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: The command-adapter generation manifest and schema validate, cross-reference command/operation/primitive/schema/conformance surfaces, and identify root `defaults` as ready for first generation.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Archive closeout and final planning summary/reconcile after #358 is closed.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Continue implementing planned work until done and feed friction back into plans.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Proceed through #356 child slices in order, starting with #358.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add the canonical command adapter generation contract and validation.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that the new contract is implementation-independent and sufficient for #359 without generating code yet.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: src/agentic_workspace/contracts, contract loaders/checks/tests, planning state/evidence/execplan/review artifacts.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: proof --changed; contract tooling checks; focused contract tests; planning-surface check; reconcile; summary.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any broad CLI rewrite, generator implementation, or package CLI migration.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Runtime command behavior except where a validation hook needs a loader.

stop_conditions.stop when: #358 is closed, committed, pushed, and #359 remains planned.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The contract cannot be made sufficient without implementing the generator.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Work expands into #359 or later child slices before #358 closes.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Contract validation or planning reconciliation fails.

context_budget.live working set: #356/#358/#359, root contracts, schema validation, conformance references, this execplan
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run and closeout_distillation before archive.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before generator implementation or broad test rewrites.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Define the command adapter generation contract.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Implementation-independent; no generator implementation; preserve runtime primitive ownership.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact file names, schema shape, and focused validation coverage.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The next best change belongs to #359 or later.

immediate_next_action: Inspect contract loader/check/test patterns, then add the minimal command-adapter contract.
invariants: Do not generate CLI adapter code in #358.
Keep adapter contracts host- and language-agnostic.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: A command adapter contract binds command shape, operation contract, runtime binding metadata, effect hints, projection hints, and conformance refs without owning primitive implementation.
open_questions_to_close: Closed: added a small command_adapter_generation.json manifest rather than expanding operation contracts with generator-only binding detail.
Closed: minimum fields are command ref, operation ref, runtime primitive sequence, schema refs, effect hints, projection hints, generation boundary, and conformance refs.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_generated_tool_conformance.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py
completion_criteria: The contract reuses existing command/operation/conformance surfaces.
The contract is sufficient to start #359 on root `defaults`.
No active execplan remains after archive closeout.

execution_run.handoff source: preflight, summary, gh issue list, #357 audit
execution_run.what happened: Added command_adapter_generation.json plus schema, validated loader access, cross-reference checks, inventory/consumption metadata, and focused tests.
execution_run.scope touched: Workspace contracts, contract tooling/checks/tests, planning state/evidence/review.
execution_run.changed surfaces: New command-adapter generation contract/schema, contract_tooling loader, contract checker, inventory, python consumption record, focused tests, planning state/evidence/review.
execution_run.validations run: preflight, summary, gh issue list, proof selector, contract tooling check, focused tests, ruff, planning doctor, planning-surface check, reconcile.
execution_run.result for continuation: #359 can generate or mechanically assemble the root `defaults` adapter from command_adapter_generation.json.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; no generated adapter code or runtime behavior changed
finished_run_review.config compliance: stayed on master and used package preflight/summary/proof/reconcile surfaces
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: Close #358; continue #359; keep #364 as dogfooding friction follow-up.

proof_report.validation proof: uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_generated_tool_conformance.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json
proof_report.proof achieved now: Contract tooling check passed, 171 focused tests passed, ruff passed, planning doctor was healthy, planning-surface drift check was clean, and reconcile was clean.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Successful local command outputs in this run; command_adapter_generation.json cross-reference checks are included in check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Continue implementing planned work until done and feed friction back into plans.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Reduce shipped executable CLI code as an interface authority surface while preserving runtime primitives.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can #359 generate a read-only adapter without broad rediscovery?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #358 defines the generation contract but intentionally does not generate an adapter.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen #358 if #359 cannot consume the command-adapter generation contract without broad rediscovery.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Canonical command-adapter generation contract for the first root `defaults` adapter.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Contract tooling, focused tests, ruff, planning doctor, planning-surface check, reconcile, and summary passed.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Closeout review records #358 as bounded-slice satisfied and #364 as dogfooding friction.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Contract inventory and python consumption metadata updated; no Memory update needed.

discard.summary: Raw implementation inspection detail is not needed after contract/check/test evidence.

continuation.summary: #359 remains open for the first generated read-only CLI adapter slice using defaults.report.cli.
#364 remains open to reduce validated contract-consumption parity double-entry friction.

memory.summary: Contract inventory and python consumption metadata updated; no Memory update needed.

issue_follow_up.summary: Command adapter generation contract complete; continue generated adapter work in #359.
